hero.question: Netanyahu out by end of 2026?
hero.prob_take:
The crowd is genuinely split at 49.5%, which means the market sees Netanyahu's political survival through 2026 as a coin flip, even with his legal troubles and coalition fragility creating real exit pressure.

The move down 5.5 points suggests conviction is breaking toward NO, but that's exactly when sharp money usually leans into the volatility: an indicted prime minister who controls the security apparatus and has survived three elections tends to be harder to remove than markets price when they're uncertain.

daily_take.headline: Netanyahu's Exit Bet Just Got Cheaper. Smart Money Disagrees.
daily_take.deck: Netanyahu out by end of 2026 dropped 5.5 points to 49.5% on $756K volume, signaling the market thinks he's staying put. But Israeli coalition dynamics are fragile and judicial pressure won't evaporate. The 50% level is a sucker's odds for even money. Smart bettors are loading up on the exit case before trial momentum shifts.
